title: GenerationPack v1
description: One generation burst prepared by a generation operator (skill side) for import into a campaign via node.import_generated. A pack is the burst's audit unit: it names the evidence delta that triggered it, the evidence snapshot it was generated from, every candidate with full provenance, AND every candidate the operator itself rejected (with reasons) — silent drops are not representable. The engine validates the pack, mints node ids, assembles idea cards deterministically from card_fields (Explain-then-Formalize: the generator authors the rationale and card fields; the engine owns formalization and its trace), and archives the pack verbatim as a campaign artifact. Packs never carry scores, posteriors, or grounding audits — evaluation authority stays with the belief layer.

trigger.description: The research-progress evidence delta that justified this burst. Kinds beyond the currently enabled set are part of the vocabulary but rejected at import (trigger_not_enabled) until their seam is exercised.

artifact_ref.description: Reference to the artifact embodying the delta (survey artifact, failed-approach ledger entry, ...). Required for every kind except manual.

evidence_snapshot.description: What the generator actually read, pinned well enough to reconstruct its input later.

parent_revisions.description: Revision of every parent node AT READ TIME (parents' mutable fields move; this pins the generator's actual input). Keys must cover the union of all candidates' parent_node_ids.

rejected_candidates.description: Candidates the operator generated but did not submit, each with its disposition. Required (may be empty) so a burst cannot silently discard work.

prompt_snapshots.description: Full prompt snapshots for this burst, archived with the pack (the pack artifact is stored verbatim). Every candidate provenance.prompt_snapshot_hash MUST equal the sha256 of one entry's content — the engine verifies this at import, which is what makes the design's reproducibility statement ("a third party can reconstruct exactly what the generator saw") checkable.

card_fields.description: Card content authored at generation time. The engine derives thesis_statement deterministically from the rationale_draft (same rule as seed import) and assembles the idea_card; claims follow the idea_card_v1 evidence discipline verbatim via $ref.

operator_family.description: Must be a family the import validator knows (arity table); unknown families are rejected — the taxonomy is committed at the validator, not the schema.

trace_inputs.description: Becomes operator_trace.inputs (engine adds trigger, the pack artifact reference, the candidate's parent_revisions, target_admission_route, dedup, and novelty_delta; supplying any of those six keys yourself is rejected as trace_key_reserved). Per-family semantic requirements (anchor for LiteratureMining, failed_approach_refs for FailureRouting, retrieval_receipts covering every evidence URI) are enforced by the import executor.

trace_params.description: Becomes operator_trace.params (the engine adds params.formalization itself; a generator-supplied formalization key is rejected).

prompt_snapshot_hash.description: MANDATORY at import (executor-enforced): sha256 of the rendered generation prompt, matching one pack.prompt_snapshots entry AND origin.prompt_hash.

dedup.description: Mechanical dedup record against ALL campaign nodes (active, waiting, archived). decision=flagged requires an explicit human override_reason or the import refuses.

intra_burst_neighbor_index.description: When the nearest neighbor is another candidate of the SAME burst (intra-burst near-duplicate), its index in candidates[]. Dedup must compare within the burst as well as against the store.

novelty_delta.description: Novelty as an auditable claim, not a score: the closest prior work plus a falsifiable statement of what is different. delta_type values parameter_tweak and rewording are declared non-novel and rejected at import.

closest_prior.description: URI or survey ref_key of the closest prior work, found by RETRIEVAL (never parametric memory).

target_admission_route.description: The admission route (idea-posterior gate) the generator believes this idea should be judged under. Triage aid only; carries no authority.

reason.description: Why the operator dropped it (dedup hit, unresolved gap anchor, freshness hit, failed anchor validation, quota, ...).
